neighborhoods.name: Alfama (Lisbon)
neighborhoods.description: Oldest neighborhood in Lisbon, clinging to the hillside below Sao Jorge Castle, with narrow alleys, fado houses, and river views
neighborhoods.character: Historic, atmospheric, steep hills, fado music, authentic Lisbon

housing.predominantType: Renovated apartments in historic buildings, many walk-up with no elevator

neighborhoods.character_notes: Tram 28 runs through. Feira da Ladra flea market on Tuesdays and Saturdays. Very steep — mobility considerations important for retirees.

neighborhoods.name: Principe Real (Lisbon)
neighborhoods.description: Trendy upscale neighborhood on a hilltop with a garden square, boutique shops, and some of Lisbon's best restaurants

housing.predominantType: Renovated apartments in 19th-century buildings, some with garden views

neighborhoods.character_notes: Jardim do Principe Real is the social center. Saturday organic market. High concentration of international residents. Most expensive Lisbon neighborhood.

neighborhoods.name: Belem (Lisbon)
neighborhoods.description: Western Lisbon district along the Tagus river with monuments, museums, waterfront promenades, and more space than central Lisbon
neighborhoods.character: Spacious, monumental, waterfront, quieter, cultural institutions

housing.predominantType: Mix of historic and modern apartments, some newer developments with river views

neighborhoods.character_notes: Jeronimos Monastery, MAAT museum, Pasteis de Belem. Tram 15E and train to central Lisbon. More residential than tourist despite landmarks.

neighborhoods.name: Cascais
neighborhoods.description: Upscale coastal town 30km west of Lisbon, once a royal summer retreat, with beaches, marina, and year-round expat community
neighborhoods.character: Coastal resort town, upscale, strong expat community, beach lifestyle with Lisbon access

housing.predominantType: Apartments and villas, mix of historic and modern, many with ocean views

neighborhoods.character_notes: Train to Lisbon center takes 40 minutes. International schools. Marina with restaurants. Guincho beach nearby for surfing. Very English-friendly.

neighborhoods.name: Lagos (Algarve)
neighborhoods.description: Historic walled town in western Algarve with dramatic cliff beaches, a lively old town, and a large international community
neighborhoods.character: Coastal historic, beach-centric, backpacker-to-retiree mix, lively year-round

housing.predominantType: Apartments in old town, villas and condos in surrounding areas

neighborhoods.character_notes: Ponta da Piedade cliffs. Marina with waterfront dining. More lively than eastern Algarve. Good year-round community. Faro airport 1 hour.

sources.title: Numbeo Cost of Living Lagos Portugal

neighborhoods.name: Tavira (Algarve)
neighborhoods.description: Elegant town in eastern Algarve straddling the Gilao River, with Moorish architecture, a Roman bridge, and nearby Ilha de Tavira beach
neighborhoods.character: Quieter, authentic, Moorish heritage, island beach, less touristy than western Algarve

housing.predominantType: Townhouses in historic center and apartments/villas in surrounding areas

neighborhoods.character_notes: More authentic Portuguese feel than Lagos or Albufeira. Ria Formosa Natural Park. Ilha de Tavira beach by ferry. Growing but still uncrowded.
